nodes.title: Epic: Conflicting slices
nodes.description:
Run two slices.

## Outcome Trace
- PLAN-CONFLICT: maintainer | CLI | run | durable report | read report | failure | integration evidence | Tasks 1 and 2, final Task 2.

## Success Criteria
- PLAN-CONFLICT passes.

nodes.title: Task 1: First slice
nodes.description:
Deliver slice one.

## Context
- Product contract: `docs/product/x.md` revision 1.
- Spec: `docs/specs/x.md`.
- Outcome IDs: PLAN-CONFLICT.
- External ref: None.
- Why this slice exists: first behavior.

## Outcome
- Actor / entry interface: maintainer runs `one`.
- Observable result: report one is shown.
- Durable result / find-again path: report is saved and readable.
- Denied/failure/recovery result: failure leaves the prior report.

## Domain Contract
- Reports are atomic.

## Files
- Modify: `src/shared.py`.

## Resources
- Allowed write set: `src/shared.py`.
- Exclusive resources: report contract.
- Capacity resources: None.

## Interfaces
- Produces: REPORT-ONE.
- Consumes: None.
- Hard ordering constraints: None.
- `one() -> Report`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `one` saves and reads a report.

## Integration Checkpoint
- Run `pytest tests/test_one.py` against the CLI and store.

## Implementation Notes
1. Test and implement slice one.
